tasks.description: Determine definitive strengths and weaknesses. Propose improvement actions.

subtasks.description: Summarize which holdings to keep, adjust, or replace based on analytical evidence.
List target attributes for new tickers to add or replace (e.g., sectors, factors, correlations).
completion_evidence: Target replacement tickers should: (1) Exhibit low to moderate correlation with existing core holdings (avoid semis, clustered REITs); (2) display strong idiosyncratic alpha/positive Sharpe; (3) enhance global and sectorial diversification (prefer non-US, overlooked developed/EM, or differentiated sectors like health care, industrials, consumer staples, select financials, or defensive cyclicals); (4) feature strong forward momentum and quality characteristics (quality, profitability, proven growth); and (5) have lower beta or differentiated risk drivers (e.g., select commodity, insurance, infra, select fintech, innovative industrial/cyclical names). Avoid pure bond/proxy ETFs and speculative high-beta single names. Prioritize global, unique factor exposures (value, quality), and thematic trends.

STRENGTHS: Portfolio has very strong risk-adjusted returns (Sharpe 2.24, Sortino 4.45, Calmar 3.8), high alpha (0.33), excellent Martin and omega ratios, win rate 59%, and robust up-capture. Star tickers include NVDA (high conviction, strong forward momentum, robust profitability), APP (highest risk/reward for aggressive capital growth), AVGO (quality, low-beta, resilient FCF), BSX (best-in-class Sharpe, stability), PLTR, SLVR, NRG, CRDO, and EMQQ for unique alpha and upside. Portfolio tilts heavily quality, with dispersion across tech, utilities, REITs, and EM/commodity ETFs.    WEAKNESSES: Beta is above 1 (1.036), high pairwise correlation clusters in semiconductors & REITs (esp. among NVDA, CRDO, MU, ARM, AVGO, MTSI), and a concentration risk (top 10 = 62%). Several positions have excessive drawdown/tail risk: DGNX (unstable, max drawdown -94%, high beta to TLT/LQD), CRDO/OKLO/FUFU/EXOD (speculative, very high beta, worst hit in credit/tech selloffs), and mediocre REITs (TRNO/FR/STAG/IIPR) with negative long-term alpha/sharpe. EQIX underperforms with negative Sharpe/long-term return. Utilities exposure could be diversified to add more idiosyncratic alpha. Credit ETFs (VCSH, IGSB) have very low return/cost, and banks are correlated.    KEEP/HOLD: NVDA, APP, BSX, AVGO, NRG, PLTR, SLVR, JPM, MU, GREK, SGDM, CEG, CMS, NLR.    ADJUST DOWN: Reduce NVDA and APP to manage single-stock/sector risk. Reduce excessive crowding in semiconductors/REITs. CUT/REPLACE: Remove DGNX, CRDO, OKLO, FUFU, EXOD, IIPR, STAG, TRNO, FR, EQIX, EMQQ, FITB, INTR, NU, VCSH, IGSB. Replace with low-correlation alpha generators, idiosyncratic winners, and more global diversification.

execution_history.reason: Heavy analytics show new portfolio achieves 36.4% ann. return, Sharpe 2.45, max drawdown -11.9%, beta 0.61 (substantially reduced). Portfolio net exposure 51%. Quality and growth factors dominate, with mild net tilt to growth. Pairwise correlations remain controlled, especially among new additions. No excessive clustering. Current setup meets high-risk tolerance, lower beta/correlation, improved resilience, and strong multi-factor style balance, so no iterative changes needed.

Current setup meets high-risk tolerance, lower beta/correlation, improved resilience, and strong multi-factor style balance, so no iterative changes needed.
